title: Flankers-NEAR
description: The Flankers-NEAR dataset comprises event-related potential (ERP) recordings collected during a visual word recognition task with flanker manipulation. Participants viewed 90 real words and 90 pseudowords presented in three flanker conditions (no flanker, identical flankers, different flankers) while seated in a sound-attenuated room. This dataset enables investigation of how orthographic context influences neural responses to written word processing.
methods_description: EEG data were collected while participants performed a visual lexical decision task. Stimuli consisted of 4-letter words and pseudowords presented on a 24-inch LCD monitor at 152 cm viewing distance. Each trial included a 1000ms purple fixation cross, 500ms white fixation cross, 150ms stimulus presentation, and response period via game controller. A total of 270 trials were presented across three flanker conditions.
